concerns.text: FR-001 explicitly requires ingesting 'NOAA/Copernicus reanalysis data' (temperature, salinity, nutrients) alongside MODIS and in-situ data. Tasks T011 and T012 describe fetching only SeaBASS (which the spec notes includes in-situ and ocean color, but not the full reanalysis driver set) and aligning it. The ingestion and alignment of the external reanalysis grids are completely missing, violating the multi-modal requirement.

FR-001 mandates a 'unified masking strategy for all missing data points' across three distinct sources (MODIS, Reanalysis, In-situ). Task T013 describes masking but assumes a single-source pipeline. There is no task to align the external reanalysis grids to the 4km MODIS grid *before* applying the unified mask, which is a prerequisite for the unified strategy.

SC-001 requires determining if the VLM R² exceeds the baseline 'by a statistically significant margin (≥ 0.05) ... using an appropriate statistical test.' Tasks T019 and T020 only describe computing and reporting metrics (RMSE, R², MAE). The specific task to perform the statistical significance test (e.g., paired t-test or bootstrap) to validate the margin is missing.

SC-005 requires reporting the 'variance in R² scores across different ocean basins' and the 'difference between the highest and lowest basin R².' Task T020 mentions generating a CSV with 'basin-stratified R² scores' but lacks an explicit task to calculate, aggregate, and output the specific variance/difference metric required by the success criterion.

The spec's 'Assumptions' section requires 'sufficient temporal overlap (≥ 10 years)' to support a stratified train/val/test split. The current tasks focus on ingestion and alignment but lack a specific task to validate this 10-year overlap or implement the specific stratified temporal split logic required by the assumption before training begins.
